Pi-hole v6 API contract capture — the source of truth for piholefake.
Captured 2026-07-10 against the official pihole/pihole:latest Docker image:
  core v6.4.3, web v6.6, FTL v6.7, docker tag 2026.07.2
(see version.json). Values sanitized to homelab.example / RFC1918.

The fake may only accept what this real API accepts; every status code and
body shape below was observed live, not inferred from docs.

--- Auth (session-based; NO Basic auth in v6) --------------------------------

POST /api/auth  {"password":"<pw>"}
  correct  -> 200, body auth-success.json
              session.sid is the token; also Set-Cookie sid=...; validity 1800s.
              A csrf token is issued but only required for COOKIE-auth clients;
              the sid HEADER path (what the driver uses) needs no csrf.
  wrong    -> 401, body auth-wrong-password.json (session.valid=false, sid null)

Authenticated requests carry the header:  sid: <sid>
  missing/bad/expired sid -> 401, body error-unauthorized.json

DELETE /api/auth (with sid header) -> 204 No Content, session destroyed
  (the same sid afterwards -> 401 error-unauthorized.json)

--- Local DNS records ("Local DNS" / dns.hosts) ------------------------------

Entries are flat "IP host" strings — dnsmasq host lines. The VALUE MUST be an
IPv4/IPv6 address; there is no metadata/comment slot on an entry.

GET /api/config/dns/hosts
  -> 200, body hosts-list.json  ({"config":{"dns":{"hosts":[...]}}, "took":...})

PUT /api/config/dns/hosts/<url-encoded "IP host">   (add one entry)
  new entry            -> 201 Created, body put-created.json ({"took":...})
  exact duplicate      -> 400, body put-duplicate.json
                          ("Item already present" / "Uniqueness of items is enforced")
  value not an IP      -> 400, body put-invalid-ip.json
                          (hint: dns.hosts[N]: neither a valid IPv4 nor IPv6 address)
  wildcard hostname    -> 400, body put-wildcard-rejected.json
                          (hint: dns.hosts[N]: invalid hostname ("*.homelab.example"))
  SAME host, DIFFERENT IP -> 201 (accepted; both entries coexist — Pi-hole does
                          not dedupe by name, only by exact string)

DELETE /api/config/dns/hosts/<url-encoded "IP host">
  entry present -> 204 No Content
  entry absent  -> 404, body delete-nonexistent.json ({"took":...})

--- Wildcards ----------------------------------------------------------------

dns.hosts REJECTS wildcard hostnames outright (400 "invalid hostname", verified
above). Wildcard answers in Pi-hole live in custom dnsmasq conf files
(address=/.../...) OUTSIDE this API — so the driver refuses wildcard hosts
loudly instead of pretending the endpoint could carry them.

--- CNAMEs --------------------------------------------------------------------

GET /api/config/dns/cnameRecords -> 200, {"config":{"dns":{"cnameRecords":[]}},...}
Separate endpoint, "cname,target[,ttl]" strings. NOT used by this driver:
crenel's Pi-hole surface is a single IP EdgeAddr via dns.hosts (a non-IP
EdgeAddr is refused at DesiredRecords).
